evidences.title: A Panorama of 2026 Bitcoin Price Forecasts: Mostly Bullish ...
evidences.content: Grayscale: New All-Time High in the First Half of the Year Asset manager Grayscale, in its latest 2026 digital-asset outlook report, predicts Bitcoin will set a new all-time high in the first half of 2026. Grayscale argues the main drivers include sustained growth in institutional investment demand and a gradually clarifying U.S. regulatory environment, which could attract more capital into the market. The firm also notes that rising macro demand for alternative stores of value (such as BTC), together with regulatory progress driving mainstream adoption, supports a bullish valuation profile for Bitcoin in 2026, and suggests the traditional “four-year cycle theory” may fail in the current cycle. Bitwise: New All-Time High Again [...] Onchain analytics firm CryptoQuant said that because Bitcoin demand growth has slowed markedly, the market may already have entered a bear phase. Based on its model, current BTC price faces downside risk: in the medium term it could first fall toward the ~$70,000 support level; if momentum continues to weaken, a deeper pullback could reach around $56,000, a level close to the so-called “realized price,” which has historically aligned with bear-market...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Predictions 2026: Analysts Forecast $60K to $250K
evidences.content: Their collective thesis holds that Bitcoin spot ETFs, broader regulatory acceptance, and the integration of public blockchains into mainstream finance have fundamentally altered Bitcoin’s market dynamics. Instead of following a predictable parabolic peak and subsequent multi-year "crypto winter," Grayscale believes Bitcoin is entering a "slow bull" phase more akin to mature assets like gold or stocks. In this view, persistent ETF inflows could override cyclical selling pressure, potentially driving Bitcoin to new all-time highs in the first half of 2026. ### Institutional Evolution: From Adoption to Structural Change [...] Bear: $78,500 12 months (Dec 2026)The "Digital Asset Market Clarity Act" has paved the way for more aggressive institutional participation, with spot BTC ETFs potentially seeing an additional $15 billion in net inflows in 2026. evidences.title: 2026 Digital Asset Outlook: Dawn of the Institutional Era | Grayscale
evidences.content: Image 6 With crypto increasingly driven by institutional capital inflows, the nature of price performance has changed. In each prior bull market, Bitcoin’s price increased by at least 1,000% over a one-year period (Exhibit 6). This time around, the maximum year-over-year price increase was about 240% (in the year to March 2024). We think the difference reflects steadier institutional buying recently compared to retail momentum chasing in past cycles. Although crypto investing involves significant risks, we believe the probability of a deep and prolonged cyclical drawdown in prices is relatively low at the time of writing. Instead, a steadier advance in prices, driven by institutional capital inflows, is more likely next year, in our view. [...] Like every other asset class, crypto is driven by a combination of fundamentals and capital flows. Commodity markets are cyclical, and crypto may have prolonged cyclical drawdowns at certain points in the future. But we don’t see it for 2026. The fundamentals look solid: we expect ongoing macro demand for alternative stores of value, and regulatory clarity to drive institutional investment into public blockchain technology. Moreover, new...

evidences.title: [PDF] CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate (BRR) Methodology Guide
evidences.content: BRR.1 Relevant Transaction: Any bitcoin versus U.S. Dollar spot trade that occurs during the TWAP Period on a Constituent Exchange and that is reported through its API to the Calculation Agent. Retrieval Time: 4:01 p.m. London time on a given Calculation Day, as given by the server clock of the Calculation Agent. TWAP Period: The 60 minute period up to and including 4:00 p.m. London time on a given Calculation Day. 1 Constituent Exchanges are published on the Administrator’s website. SUMMARY DESCRIPTION © 2015 - 2017 CRYPTO FACILITIES LTD. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED. PATENT PENDING. PLEASE REFER TO THE NOTICE AND DISCLAIMER AT THE END OF THIS DOCUMENT. It is representative of the bitcoin trading activity on Constituent Exchanges and is geared towards resilience and replicability. evidences.title: 2026 Crypto Market Outlook
evidences.content: Below is a short summary of our views. ## Market Trends Cautiously optimistic outlook: We believe the U.S. economy remains resilient, with rising labor productivity offering a buffer against slowing headline data. As such, we think the crypto market setup in 1H26 rhymes more with “1996” than “1999” (i.e., we’re constructive going into next year), but we acknowledge the uncertainty band remains wide. Image 9: unnamed (87) Regulatory Progress: We saw landmark U.S. and global regulatory advances in 2025, which enabled new spot crypto ETFs, digital asset treasuries (DATs), and broader institutional participation. In our view, clearer global frameworks will continue to change how institutions approach strategy, risk, and compliance in 2026. Image 10: unnamed (88) [...] Key takeaways Crypto markets are poised for transformative growth in 2026, as clearer regulation and accelerating institutional integration deepen crypto’s role in the core financial system. Key themes we're focusing on in 2026 include the macro landscape, technological transformations, regulatory progress, tokenization, stablecoins, and more. Written by 󰍺 David Duong, CFA - Global Head of Research Colin Basco - Resear...

evidences.title: CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate and Index: How to Invest - Bitget
evidences.content: ## Defining the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate (BRR) The CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate (BRR) is a daily reference rate that provides the US Dollar price of one Bitcoin as of 4:00 p.m. London time. It is designed to provide a standardized price that major financial institutions can use for the settlement of futures contracts and the calculation of Net Asset Value (NAV) for investment funds. [...] CME CF Bitcoin Real-Time Index (BRTI) | | | | Update Frequency | Once daily (4:00 p.m. London time) | Every second (Real-time) | | Primary Use Case | Settlement of Futures, NAV for ETFs | Intraday trading, Portfolio marking | | Calculation Method | Volume-weighted median (1-hour window) | Aggregated real-time order books | | Regulatory Status | UK BMR Compliant | UK BMR Compliant | As shown in the table above, the two products serve complementary roles. The BRR acts as the "official" daily price for institutional settlement, while the BRTI offers the high-frequency data required for active market participation and risk management. ## How to Invest Using CME CF Benchmarks [...] ## Regulatory Oversight and Industry Standards The credibility of the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate and CME CF Bi...

evidences.title: CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate: Explainer Video - YouTube
evidences.content: The CME CF Bitcoin reference rate or BRR is a once a day benchmark index [music] price for one Bitcoin denominated in US dollars that aggregates [music] trade data from multiple Bitcoin USD markets operated by major cryptocurrency exchanges in continuous calculation since 2016. [music] The BRR is a registered benchmark regulated by the UK FCA. [music] It is the premier index for regulated Bitcoin financial products and is used by leading financial institutions including CME Group to settle their Bitcoin futures contracts. The BRR is also the NAV benchmark to Bitcoin ETFs and ETPs from issuers such as [music] Black Rockck, Evolve and Bitwise Europe. evidences.title: BRRNY - CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate - CF Benchmarks
evidences.content: RR New York New York Loading Chart ## About The CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate - New York Variant (BRRNY) is a once a day benchmark index price for Bitcoin that aggregates trade data from multiple Bitcoin-USD markets operated by major cryptocurrency exchanges that conform to the CME CF Constituent Exchange Criteria. It is the pre-eminent index price for Bitcoin risk settlement that is synchronized to the traditional US financial market close of 1600 New York Time. The Index is a Registered Benchmark under UK BMR and as such is a Third Country benchmark under the UK BMR Regime. The index was launched on February 28th 2022. Bloomberg: BRRNY ## Index Series CME CF Single Asset Series ## Derivatives & Structured Products ## Referencing Products [...] ## Usage & Licensing If you require access to real time or historic data for this index to power a product or service or are interested in licensing the index for the creation of a financial product, investment fund or derivative instrument please contact(/cdn-cgi/l/email-protection#98f4f1fbfdf6ebf1f6ffd8fbfefafdf6fbf0f5f9eaf3ebb6fbf7f5) ## Documentation CME CF Single Asset Series (6) : CME CF Reference Rates Methodology Download CME CF...

evidences.title: FAQ: CME CF Single-Asset Cryptocurrency Benchmarks
evidences.content: CME CF Reference Rates are calculated by aggregating the executed trade flow of a particular cryptocurrency pair on major spot exchanges during a specific one-hour calculation window. This window is partitioned into 12 five-minute intervals, with the final reference rate being the equally weighted average of the volume-weighted median trade price of all partitions. Details of the methodology can be found in the CME CF Cryptocurrency Reference Rates Methodology Guide. Back to Top ### 5. Why were these reference rates created? [...] Back to Top ### 3. What are CME CF Reference Rates? CME CF Reference Rates aggregate trade flow of major cryptocurrency spot exchanges during specific calculation windows into a once-a-day reference rate of the U.S. dollar or euro price of the respective cryptocurrency. Calculation rules are geared toward maximum transparency and real-time replicability in underlying spot markets. Back to Top ### 4. What methodology is used to calculate reference rates? [...] The rate is calculated by aggregating trade flow of major cryptocurrency spot exchanges using U.S. dollar prices. This is a non-tradeable benchmark. Back to Top ## OVERVIEW ### 2. evidences.title: Suitability Analysis of the CME CF Bitcoin Reference Rate - Nasdaq
evidences.content: Calculation Methodology The BRRNY calculation methodology aggregates transactions of Bitcoin in U.S. dollars conducted only on the most liquid markets for which data are publicly available, and operated by exchanges that meet the CME CF Constituent Exchange Criteria. The list of Constituent Exchanges and information about changes to its composition are available at the following URL: The full methodology is also available here: The methodology can be summarised thus: [...] Representative: Bitcoin-USD markets that are operated by the CME CF Constituent Exchanges during 15:00 to 16:00 New York Time are liquid, with sufficient volume of trading to represent the market in a robust manner. Resistant to Manipulation: BRRNY Constituent Exchange Criteria ensure that it takes input data only from cryptocurrency exchanges that exhibit fair and orderly behaviour, where trading shows strong price correlations between each other. On top of this, the methodology BRRNY employs nullifies the effects of any manipulation, and the Administrator’s policies and processes regarding surveillance ensure that any manipulation is detected. [...] The methodology can be summarised thus: Transactions conduc...

evidences.title: Introduction to Bitcoin Reference Rate - CME Group
evidences.content: ### Bitcoin Real Time Index (BRTI) BRTI is a real-time index of the U.S. dollar price of one bitcoin, published once per second, 24 hours a day, 365 days per year. This index provides real time transparency to the U.S. dollar price of bitcoin. ### Bitcoin Reference Rate (BRR) BRR is a daily reference rate of the U.S. dollar price of one bitcoin as of 4 p.m. London time. Each day, the BRR aggregates the trade flow of major bitcoin spot exchanges during a specific one-hour calculation window. This one-hour window is then partitioned into 12, five-minute intervals, where the BRR is calculated as the equally-weighted average of the volume-weighted medians of all 12 partitions. This BRR calculation ensures tradability and replicability in the underlying spot markets. [...] ### Oversight of the CME CF Bitcoin Pricing Products The independent oversight committee is responsible for overseeing the scope of the Bitcoin Reference Rate by developing a code of conduct for the participants and regularly reviewing the practice, standards and definition of the reference rate to ensure it remains relevant and retains its integrity. To learn more visit cmegroup.com/bitcoin. ## Test your knowledge...
